76238.subject: Data & Insights: Strategy's Collapse, RWA's Rise
76238.body:
The Data and Insights newsletter brings the highlights of the week from The Block’s Data Dashboard

( https://www.theblock.co/data/crypto-markets/spot )

Gm data enthusiasts! What a way to wrap up the World Cup. Now that we're no longer glued to our screens, our team recently set up a new newsletter titled " Tokenization Briefing ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters )." With trillions of dollars of real-world assets migrating onto blockchain rails we are introducing a way for readers to keep up to date with market insights and visual analyses on what matters in the world of Tokenization. Sign up *here* ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters ).

This week, we’re taking a look at altcoin ETFs and Strategy mNAV. We’ll also look at tokenized assets, Robinhood, and prediction market data. So grab a drink, take a seat, and let's dive in.

1: Altcoin Alternatives

( https://www.theblock.co/data/etfs/etf-comparison/spot-etf-market-share-by-coin-excluding-btc-and-eth )

Chart via The Block's Data Dashboard

*Key Insights* provided by The Block Research

* While we’ve been focusing on Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs, altcoin ETFs have continued to trade under most people's radar. Solana and Hyperliquid ETFs have combined to account for nearly 80% of non-BTC and ETH ETF volume, with Hyperliquid ETFs having launched only 2 months ago.

* Solana boasts an impressive $904M AUM, while the Hyperliquid suite has managed to attract $350M in net inflows. Both figures represent approximately 2% of each token's market cap.
* Bitcoin, by comparison, has nearly 9% of its market cap held in ETF products. The gap suggests that altcoin ETFs may benefit further as they gain investor acceptance.
* Alternatively, this could be reflected in BTC’s nearly 2-year head start rather than a ceiling that alts are guaranteed to close.

* These flows are likely skewed by investor type. SOL and HYPE sit further down the risk curve with less regulatory precedent and higher volatility, so they're attracting allocators with higher risk tolerance. Bitcoin and Ethereum, however, benefit from the anchoring of investors who tend to be stickier and more passive.

* As projects such as Hyperliquid and Solana continue to engage with regulatory bodies and build out RWA rails, this may open the door to investors interested in that exposure.

See the Interactive Chart ( https://www.theblock.co/data/etfs/etf-comparison/spot-etf-market-share-by-coin-excluding-btc-and-eth )

2: Strategic Conclusions

( https://www.theblock.co/data/treasuries/bitcoin-treasuries/strategy-mstr-premium-discount-to-nav )

Chart via The Block's Data Dashboard

*Key Insights* provided by The Block Research

* MSTR is down roughly 38% this year, with this market cap now sitting at just under $35B. This comes as its Bitcoin stack is worth $54.5B, or 0.63x when you divide market cap by the value of its BTC.

* This is a simple mNAV and fails to capture the leverage that is included on top of

76237.subject: Boomers have the good life, but it could be longer
76237.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDN9AAcAAAAABDudPcrwR9Y368m2yREITlu8P6wDvmk4xxsztl_t50krAyHDqVfVw0SSZabYJoFtu_SRc-a_u0XEhUAuJSU7ou15sZyfEwkjUHSu3ay__KVT8Q 

July 20th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDN9AAcAAAAABDudPcrxBQqtdXnzkPfFbvSvANghIY1O2NAhZKVE3E_P1LYQitXy-VgGu7CMVSOA_WubN7iK-TGphtoeLhkIFdj4gzRhARpPGpxtOhBNJzjcPQ 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDN9AAcAAAAABDudPcrydEpJ85MxrLxqvOo3DqiUdVJJyuPBhPzFZbxNQlFYcjwOv9OnEhm5JpCH6EU9wwxi74HwaMZlOO0SvP2nHPUvD5s_VOctX93oYjD_Ow 


 


 
 

Stephanie Studer

Senior digital editor



 Something is wrong with American life expectancy. Lifespans had been on a joyous forward march for over a century. After 2010 they stalled. And one generation has lost out more than any other: the boomers. A single chart traces the arc of their lives (and all of ours), so our visual team 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDN9AAcAAAAABDudPcrz6TwLzREdAQRwZ-TF1t3OczQKj8iQ_CXeJkpd0K90AlVXt3UfE9EXZzmeX8tpS1kDdXNGC_QklKz4NPrmuRzQ9ykxZaWoomsb 
tells you this story ,
 start to finish, in one gorgeous graphic.


 Elsewhere: many Swedes view their country as a sensible, cosmopolitan democracy. Yet it has also dramatically curbed immigration over the past decade. A general election in September will test whether

🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Global regulatory shifts and institutional maneuvering dominate today’s mood. Nigeria and Singapore refine their crypto frameworks, while Grayscale proposes staking rewards for major asset trusts amid rising macroeconomic tensions. 🏛️Grayscale Staking Payout Proposal Could Reshape Ethereum And Solana Trusts Grayscale has proposed a new staking payout mechanism for its Ethereum and Solana investment trusts. This structural shift could fundamentally change how these institutional products generate value for shareholders through on-chain rewards. See details 🇳🇬Nigeria’s Tinubu signs executive order creating a virtual asset council to regulate crypto President Tinubu has signed an executive order establishing a Virtual Asset Council to govern Nigeria's 25 million crypto users. The framework seeks to unify regulation and provide clarity for one of the world's most active digital asset markets. Read full story 🇸🇬Singapore considers tax cuts for hedge funds and asset managers in bid to outflank Hong Kong The Monetary Authority of Singapore is negotiating aggressive tax cuts, including potential 0% carried-interest rates, to attract digital asset managers. The move is a strategic attempt to secure the city-state's position as Asia's primary crypto hub. Read full story 🛡️Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong clarifies social media posts are not token endorsements CEO Brian Armstrong issued a clarification stating his social media activity should not be viewed as investment advice. The statement followed a massive 3,000% spike in a $BRIAN memecoin after Armstrong updated his profile picture. Read full story 📉Exodus expects $10M to $13M in annual savings after cutting 25% of its workforce Software wallet provider Exodus is reducing its staff by a quarter to streamline operations and save millions in annual costs. The company is pivoting its core strategy toward stablecoin payments and card issuance services. Read full story ⛽US gasoline prices climb back above $4 as Strait of Hormuz tensions rattle oil markets US gas prices have surged past $4 per gallon as geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz push crude prices higher. These inflationary pressures are being closely watched by crypto investors as they impact broader risk-on sentiment. Read full story 🤖Neo emerges from stealth with $100M to secure enterprise AI s

76232.subject: Saylor stops buying Bitcoin, opposes new Bitcoin improvement
76232.body:
Today’s newsletter is brought to you by **[Choose Rich Live](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**[!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)

Our very own Nick O’Neill is has launched a new stream covering crypto, finance, AI, tech, and the stories shaping the internet economy.

If you already follow Nick, you know what to expect: sharp takes, market chaos, founder brain, and the occasional banger you’ll see clipped on the timeline five minutes later.

Episodes will be streaming live on YouTube, so make sure you’re subscribed to catch the next one as it happens.

**[Subscribe to Choose Rich Live!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**

———————————————————————————

## Top Stories

**BTC** $64,877 (0.7%) | **ETH** $1,885 (0.8%) | **SOL** $77 (1.1%)

* Michael Saylor voices opposition to [BIP 110](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2078613110190149752) — Strategy adds [$225M to cash](https://x.com/Strategy/status/2079175082996306153?s=20) reserve, doesn’t purchase additional Bitcoin

* US launches [new strikes](https://x.com/DeItaone/status/2078607071918850338) against Iran after 2 US service members were [killed in Jordan](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2078541017591029875)

* [Cobie speaks out](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2078452204009328833) after taking over lead of Base App

* Tether has until July 2028 to meet [GENIUS Act requirements](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2078846108864790941)

* Anthropic adds [Claude Fable 5 access](https://x.com/claudeai/status/2078302415804379218) to max and enterprise plans

* Cardano completes first [hard fork](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2078830684509209074) approved entirely through onchain governance

* Brian Armstrong changes [PFP to a Cryptopunk](https://x.com/punk9059/status/2078355396054663379) after purchasing multiple punks

* FBI arrests hacker who used [fake steam games](https://x.com/WatcherGuru/status/2078507036250112117) to drain users’ wallets

* Fomo weekly revenue hits [new ATH](https://x.com/DegenerateNews/status/2079176974753218970?s=20)

* Tesla and Google will report [earnings this Wednesday](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2078827991157592094)

* Kimi K3 outperforms GPT 5.6 for [cybersecurity tasks](https://x.com/cramforce/status/2078574147333152957) in performance and price — New subscriptions [are paused](https://x.com/Kimi_Moonshot/status/2078855608565207130) due to overwhelming demand

———————————————————————————


———

You are reading a plain text version of this post. For the best experience, copy and paste this link in your browser to view the post online:
https://www.dailybones.com/p/saylor-stops-buying-bitcoin-opposes-new-bitcoin-improvement
